subject: [PATCH v6 10/10] selftests/dax: add dax/kmem hotplug sysfs regression test

2026-06-30.patch_summary: This patch adds a selftest to verify the functionality of dax/kmem hotplug sysfs attributes. The test checks that transitioning a kmem-backed dax device between 'unplugged', 'online' and 'online_movable' states works as expected, including idempotence and correct memory management. It also includes a destructive test to ensure force-unbinding does not deadlock. The test discovers an existing kmem-bound dax device or skips if none are available. The patch covers specific regression testing for the online -> unplug -> online_movable -> unplug sequence.

reviews.author: Richard Cheng
reviews.summary: Reviewer Richard Cheng noted that the current implementation picks the first kmem-bound dax device and runs online/offline cycles on it, which may cause issues if the selected device is a real device with production usage, as offlining movable memory will migrate all in-use pages. He suggested either gating the destructive parts behind an opt-in environment variable or finding a testable backend device instead of picking the first one.
reviews.raw_body:
It picks the first kmem-bound dax device and runs online/offline cycles on it.
If the selected device is a real device with production usage, offlining movable
memory will migrate all in-use pages.

Could the destructive parts be gated behind an opt-in, e.g. an environment var ?
Or find the testable backend device instead of just picking the first one?

Best regards,
Richard Cheng.

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the test should be able to run on a real device, agreed to modify the patch to include an environment variable for this purpose.
reviews.raw_body:
I think probably easiest to just include an environment variable, as
some people may in fact want to run this on a real device. 

Will work this in.

Thank you
~Gregory

2026-07-09.patch_summary: This patch adds a selftest to the Linux kernel's dax (Direct Access eXecution) feature, specifically targeting the kmem hotplug sysfs interface. The test checks that the 'state' attribute of a kmem-backed dax device correctly transitions between 'unplugged', 'online', and 'online_movable' states, and also verifies that force-unbinding does not deadlock. Additionally, it includes a regression test to ensure that re-onlineing after unplug correctly offlines and removes memory.
